name: Classic Barbecued Spareribs
recipeIngredient: 2 lb Spareribs (have the butcher trim the fat and discard the breastbone)
recipeInstructions:
-------------------------MARINADE-------------------------
4 tablespoons Chicken broth
1/4 cup Soy sauce
3 tablespoons Honey or corn syrup
3 tablespoons Hoisin sauce
2 tablespoons Wine vinegar
2 tablespoons Dry sherry or Shaoshing wine
1 teaspoon Sugar
2 cloves (medium) Garlic, chopped finely
1/2 teaspoon Cinnamon powder (optional)
(optional) 5-spice powder
-----------------------DIPPING SAUCE-----------------------
Plum sauce
Chopped scallions
Preheat the oven to 375 degrees F. Trim any excess fat from the ribs and place them in a long shallow dish. In a mixing bowl, combine the marinade ingredients; if using 5-spice powder, add it to the mixture through a tea strainer so it spreads out over the mixture. Mix well and pour over the ribs. Let marinate at room temperature for 3 hours or about 6 hours in the refrigerator. Baste the ribs every hour or so, turning them over.
TO ROAST THE RIBS:
Remove the ribs from the marinade. Add the 1/4 cup plum sauce to the marinade; reserve it for basting. Hook metal drapery hooks through both ends of each rack of ribs at the top and hang each one as if hanging a hammock, suspended from the bars of one of the oven racks. Set the oven rack to the highest position in the oven. On the floor of the oven or on another oven rack set at the lowest position in the oven, place a baking pan half filled with water to collect drippings and prevent smoking. Line the baking pan with aluminum foil before putting the water in for easy cleaning.
Roast the spareribs for 45 minutes in the preheated oven. Baste them with the reserved basting sauce using a pastry brush and carefully pull out the oven rack to facilitate the basting process. Turn the oven up to 450 degrees F and roast for another 15 minutes. By this time, the spareribs should be a rich golden brown with crisp edges. Baste again, then remove them from the oven. Place the ribs on a cutting board and remove the drapery hooks. Serve at room temperature with dipping sauce. Makes 6 to 8 appetizer servings.
